([bcdacfa1b3df](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/bcdacfa1b3df))
- **Agent event bus is not the requested run-trace schema:** AgentEventPayload still carries runId, seq, stream, ts, generic data, and optional sessionKey; it does not define parentRunId, taskId, stepId, handoffType, status, latencyMs, or a versioned event-source/type vocabulary. ([`src/infra/agent-events.ts:102`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/ecb6da9289b1/src/infra/agent-events.ts#L102), [ecb6da9289b1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/ecb6da9289b1))

([`src/agents/cache-trace.ts:26`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/ecb6da9289b1/src/agents/cache-trace.ts#L26), [ecb6da9289b1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/ecb6da9289b1))

([`src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts:194`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/ecb6da9289b1/src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts#L194), [ecb6da9289b1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/ecb6da9289b1))

Likely related people:

(role: trace/runtime adjacent contributor; confidence: high; commits: [c76646adb135](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/c76646adb135), [1a13c34f5bc8](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1a13c34f5bc8), [bcdacfa1b3df](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/bcdacfa1b3df000ce04e3ce26bbe3051e56da3e8); files: `src/agents/cache-trace.ts`, `src/infra/diagnostic-trace-context.ts`, `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts`)
- **steipete:** History shows introduction and recent maintenance of structured agent event surfaces, diagnostics timeline surfaces, and ACP relay handling that overlap the requested step/tool/run timeline contract. (role: agent event and diagnostics contributor; confidence: high; commits: [996dccb19ca1](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/996dccb19ca193080017d8920c1ce51c63a8fcf8), [af81ee9fee8b](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/af81ee9fee8b), [aaadf721e360](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/aaadf721e360); files: `src/infra/agent-events.ts`, `src/infra/diagnostics-timeline.ts`, `src/agents/acp-spawn-parent-stream.ts`)

(role: task-flow adjacent contributor; confidence: high; commits: [2fa4c7cc6174](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/2fa4c7cc6174acbf6caa09309f6f4838df7c538a), [7590c22db7d2](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/7590c22db7d2), [0787266637fd](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/0787266637fd); files: `src/tasks/task-executor.ts`, `src/tasks/task-registry.ts`, `src/tasks/task-registry.types.ts`)

Body:
```markdown
## Summary

Keep the TUI visibly busy while a run is waiting on a follow-up event after `sessions_yield`.

Before this change, the TUI could appear idle even though work was still effectively in flight. The gap showed up when the final assistant message contained the `sessions_yield` follow-up context marker: the run had ended intentionally, but the UI status dropped back to an idle-looking state.

## What changed

- add `awaiting follow-up` as a busy TUI activity state
- detect the `sessions_yield` follow-up marker in final assistant output
- preserve `awaiting follow-up` when reloading session history
- clear stale `awaiting follow-up` state back to `idle` when history no longer contains the marker
- add focused tests for both live event handling and history restore behavior

## Files

- `src/tui/tui.ts`
- `src/tui/tui-event-handlers.ts`
- `src/tui/tui-session-actions.ts`
- `src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts`
- `src/tui/tui-session-actions.test.ts`

## Validation

- direct smoke harness passed for:
  - final event -> `awaiting follow-up`
  - history restore -> `awaiting follow-up`
  - stale follow-up state -> `idle`
- local lint/format checks passed on the touched files

## Notes

A focused local Vitest run hit a Node internal assertion in this environment, so the behavior was additionally checked with a direct harness against the patched TUI logic.
```

github_context:
GitHub item:
- Repository: openclaw/openclaw
- Type: github_issue
- Number: 83921
- URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/83921
- Title: Benchmark spawner scripts use bare "node" instead of process.execPath, risking version mismatch
- State: CLOSED
- Author: davinci282828
- Labels: P2, clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear, clawsweeper:queueable-fix, clawsweeper:current-main-repro, issue-rating: 🦀 challenger crab

Body:
```markdown
**Severity:** medium / **Confidence:** high / **Category:** bug
**Triage:** confirmed-bug
**Detected against:** openclaw v2026.5.18 (latest stable at time of scan, 2026-05-18)
**Tooling:** clawpatch 0.3.0 + acpx/claude-sonnet-4-5 via [Brad Mills protocol](https://x.com/bradmillscan/status/2056377217437909178)

## Evidence

- `scripts/test-cli-startup-bench-budget.mjs:88-97` (resolveCurrentReportPath)

```
const run = spawnSync("node", args, {
    cwd: process.cwd(),
    stdio: "inherit",
    env: process.env,
  });
```

- `scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs:46-52` (None)

```
const run = spawnSync("node", args, {
  cwd: process.cwd(),
  stdio: "inherit",
  env: process.env,
});
```

## Reasoning
Both scripts call spawnSync("node", ...) which resolves "node" through PATH. In environments using version managers (nvm, fnm, volta, asdf), the PATH-resolved "node" may differ from the Node.js instance currently executing the script (process.execPath). This means the bench runner and the fixture updater could execute bench-cli-startup.ts on a different Node version than intended, producing measurements that are not comparable to the baseline or to what CI uses. bench-cli-startup.ts itself correctly uses process.execPath when spawning the CLI under test, making the inconsistency more subtle.

## Reproduction
Use nvm to set node 20 as default, then run the scripts while nvm shell activation points to node 22 — the spawned bench will run on node 20 while the parent runs on node 22.

## Recommendation
Replace `spawnSync("node", args, ...)` with `spawnSync(process.execPath, args, ...)` in both scripts, matching the pattern used in bench-cli-startup.ts and check-cli-startup-memory.mjs.

## Why existing tests miss this
No tests exist for these scripts. The version mismatch only becomes visible in polyglot Node environments.

## Minimum fix scope
Change the first argument of spawnSync from "node" to process.execPath in scripts/test-cli-startup-bench-budget.mjs (resolveCurrentReportPath) and scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs.

---
_Standardized clawpatch finding. Persistent in v2026.5.18 (not resolved by upgrading from v2026.5.12). Finding ID: `fnd_sig-feat-cli-command-23edfe2554-_2dbd4973be`._
```

**Summary**
Keep open: the benchmark wrapper scripts still use a PATH-resolved `node` child process on current main, and a no-PATH current-main probe reproduces the same resolver defect without needing a full version-manager setup.

Reproducibility: yes. A current-main run of the updater with an absolute parent Node and `PATH=/nonexistent` exits 1 because the wrapper tries to resolve bare `node` from PATH.

**Next step**
Safe repair candidate: the fix is limited to two benchmark wrapper spawns plus focused validation, with no product or config decision required.


<details>
<summary>Review details</summary>

Best possible solution:

Change the two wrapper spawns to `process.execPath` and add a narrow regression check proving the wrappers keep working when the parent Node is absolute but `node` is unavailable or different on PATH.

Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue?

Yes. A current-main run of the updater with an absolute parent Node and `PATH=/nonexistent` exits 1 because the wrapper tries to resolve bare `node` from PATH.

Is this the best way to solve the issue?

Yes. Replacing the two child-process command strings with `process.execPath` is the narrow maintainable fix and matches the existing benchmark harness behavior.

Label justifications:

- `P2`: This is a real tooling correctness bug with limited blast radius to benchmark/update scripts rather than a core runtime outage.

Acceptance criteria:

- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/cli-startup-bench-spawner.test.ts
- node_bin="$(command -v node)"; PATH=/nonexistent "$node_bin" scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs --out /tmp/openclaw-cli-startup-bench-review.json --preset startup --runs 1 --warmup 0 --timeout-ms 10000
- node_bin="$(command -v node)"; PATH=/nonexistent "$node_bin" scripts/test-cli-startup-bench-budget.mjs --preset startup --runs 1 --warmup 0 --timeout-ms 10000 --max-duration-regression-pct 100000 --max-first-output-regression-pct 100000 --max-rss-regression-pct 100000

What I checked:

- **Current budget wrapper uses bare node:** `resolveCurrentReportPath()` builds the benchmark args, then calls `spawnSync("node", args, { cwd: process.cwd(), stdio: "inherit", env: process.env })`, so the child executable is resolved through PATH rather than the parent Node executable. ([`scripts/test-cli-startup-bench-budget.mjs:108`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/e9989f3a92e5/scripts/test-cli-startup-bench-budget.mjs#L108), [e9989f3a92e5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/e9989f3a92e5))
- **Current update wrapper uses bare node:** The fixture updater has the same `spawnSync("node", args, ...)` pattern before writing the benchmark fixture. ([`scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs:67`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/e9989f3a92e5/scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs#L67), [e9989f3a92e5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/e9989f3a92e5))
- **Adjacent benchmark harness already uses the stable executable:** `bench-cli-startup.ts` spawns the CLI under test with `process.execPath`, matching the issue's proposed contract for benchmark child processes. ([`scripts/bench-cli-startup.ts:580`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/e9989f3a92e5/scripts/bench-cli-startup.ts#L580), [e9989f3a92e5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/e9989f3a92e5))
- **Dependency contract supports the report:** Node's child_process documentation says command lookup uses `options.env.PATH`, while the process documentation says `process.execPath` is the absolute executable that started the current Node process. ([nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org/api/child_process.html))
- **Current-main reproduction:** Running the update wrapper with the parent Node invoked by absolute path and `PATH=/nonexistent` exits 1 before running the benchmark, which is the expected failure mode for the current bare `node` child lookup. ([`scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs:67`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/e9989f3a92e5/scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs#L67), [e9989f3a92e5](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/e9989f3a92e5))
- **Shipped behavior also contains the pattern:** The latest stable tag in the provided context, `v2026.5.18`, contains both wrapper scripts with the same bare `node` child spawn. ([`scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs:67`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/50a2481652b6/scripts/test-update-cli-startup-bench.mjs#L67), [50a2481652b6](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/50a2481652b6))

Greptile Summary

This PR narrows gateway hot-reload scope by introducing account-scoped channel restarts. Previously, any config change under `channels..*` triggered a full channel restart; the change teaches the reload planner to detect paths matching `channels..accounts..*` and instead restart only the affected account via the existing `stopChannel(name, accountId)` / `startChannel(name, accountId)` lifecycle hooks. Full-channel restarts are preserved for channel-global config changes, and a post-processing cleanup step ensures a mixed batch (account-scoped + channel-global for the same channel) always collapses back to a full restart — so the new path is strictly narrower, never broader. Key changes: - `GatewayReloadPlan` gains a `restartChannelAccounts: Map>` field; `buildGatewayReloadPlan` populates it from scoped paths and clears conflicting entries when a full restart is also planned for the same channel. - `createGatewayReloadHandlers` wires up account-scoped restarts before the existing full-channel loop; the `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS` / `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS` guards continue to apply to both paths. - Four new unit tests in `config-reload.test.ts` cover each decision branch, and an integration test in `server.reload.test.ts` confirms `stopChannel`/`startChannel` are called with the correct `accountId`. - All pre-existing plan fixtures in `server.reload.test.ts` are updated with `restartChannelAccounts: new Map()` to stay in sync with the type change.

Confidence Score: 5/5

- This PR is safe to merge; changes are narrowly scoped, logically correct, and well covered by tests. - The plan-building logic correctly handles all four cases (account-scoped only, channel-global only, mixed, multi-account) via clear branching and a post-loop deduplication step. The handler wiring is additive and consistent with existing patterns. No regressions are introduced to full-channel restart behavior, and test coverage directly exercises the new code paths end-to-end. - No files require special attention. automation cannot supply the external contributor's live gateway proof. **Security** Needs attention: Account-scoped credential reloads can bypass the current `secrets.reload` restart and rollback path unless the new target map is integrated there. **Review findings** - [P2] Route account restarts through reload deferral — `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts:123-126` - [ [truncated] ``` Changed files (4): ```text src/gateway/config-reload-plan.ts src/gateway/config-reload.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts ```
